title: Festuca glaucoidea
taxonomy: Festuca glaucoidea is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is further divided into the genus Festuca, which includes various species of fescue grasses. Within the genus Festuca, F. glaucoidea is placed in the subgenus Festuca. The subgenus Festuca is a group of temperate grasses native to the Northern Hemisphere. This classification suggests that F. glaucoidea is a temperate grass species native to the Northern Hemisphere.

intro: Festuca glaucoidea, also known as blue fescue grass or blue fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Festuca glaucoidea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
